question.text: What intermediaries does Section 11(2) of the SEBI Act specifically include in SEBI's regulatory powers?

answer.text: Stock brokers, sub-brokers, merchant bankers, portfolio managers, investment advisers, depositories, custodians, and credit rating agencies, among others
Only mutual funds and insurance companies
Only foreign institutional investors and domestic institutional investors

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 11(2) lists a comprehensive set of intermediaries including stock brokers, sub-brokers, share transfer agents, bankers to an issue, trustees of trust deeds, registrars, merchant bankers, underwriters, portfolio managers, investment advisers, depositories, custodians, FIIs, and CRAs. **The Trap:** SEBI's regulatory scope is very broad — it covers virtually all securities market intermediaries, not just a narrow category. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If you work in the securities market, you're likely under SEBI's regulatory umbrella. The "such other intermediaries as notified" clause gives SEBI flexibility to bring new categories under regulation.
